content: Autonomous curiosity-driven intrinsic motivation mechanisms (from ICM-based agent research) can be repurposed to actively curate and prune growing long-term memory stores in 2024-2025 SOTA active learning agents, replacing fixed-size memory eviction policies that currently discard high-impact under-explored information. This intrinsic curiosity signal prioritizes retaining ambiguous, prediction-challenging memory entries that drive future active learning gains, instead of just discarding the least frequently accessed entries.
